---
title: "NAFOSTED proposal was funded"
description: "Enhancing the Performance of 6G Open RAN Integrating Edge Computing and Network Slicing"
author: "Dinh Nguyen"
date: "2024-02-01"
image: nafosted2024.png
draft: false
categories:
  - funding
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
    toc: false
comments:
  utterances: 
    repo: https://github.com/icclabo/icc
    label: nafosted2024
---

Our project, entitled Enhancing the Performance of 6G Open RAN Integrating Edge Computing and Network Slicing, was funded by the National Foundation for Science and Technology (NAFOSTED).

The integration of Open RAN and Edge Computing plays a crucial role in enhancing the performance of 6G systems. By enabling flexible, software-defined architectures and bringing computation closer to end users, this integration can significantly reduce latency and improve real-time service delivery. However, it also introduces challenges such as increased system complexity, resource management difficulties, and the need for efficient coordination between distributed network and computing components. To address these issues, intelligent resource allocation, AI-driven optimization, and dynamic network slicing are essential to ensure scalability, efficiency, and robustness in next-generation wireless networks.


<!--
> Intelligence, Computing & Communication Lab: Advancing an Interdisciplinary
Program of Research

{{< pdf Girard_TT_Slides.pdf width=600 height=400 image_width=600 image_border=1 image=firstslide.png >}}
-->
